name: Mexico
note: Co-hosts — opened the tournament at Estadio Azteca with a win over South Africa.

name: South Korea
note: Opened with a 2–1 comeback win over Czechia.

name: Czechia
note: Lost their opener 2–1 to South Korea.

name: South Africa
note: Lost the tournament opener 2–0 to Mexico.

name: Canada
note: Co-hosts — earned their first-ever World Cup point with a 1–1 draw vs Bosnia.

name: Bosnia & Herzegovina
note: Drew 1–1 with Canada in their first match back since 2014.

name: Switzerland
note: Faces Qatar to open their campaign.

name: Qatar
note: First World Cup appearance as a non-host nation.

name: Brazil
note: Chase a record-extending 6th title, opening against Morocco.

name: Scotland
note: First World Cup since 1998 — face Haiti in their opener.

name: Morocco
note: The 2022 semi-finalists open against Brazil at MetLife Stadium.

name: Haiti
note: Return to the World Cup for the first time in over 50 years.

name: USA
note: Co-hosts — opened with a 4–1 win over Paraguay.

name: Australia
note: Face Türkiye in their opening match.

name: Türkiye
note: Face Australia in their opening match.

name: Paraguay
note: Lost their opener 4–1 to hosts USA.

name: Germany
note: Four-time champions enter as one of the heavyweight contenders.

name: Ecuador
note: A tough physical test for Germany in Group E.

name: Côte d'Ivoire
note: Reigning AFCON champions making their fourth World Cup appearance.

name: Curaçao
note: The smallest nation ever to qualify (population under 150,000).

name: Netherlands
note: Perennial near-misses, looking for a first-ever title.

name: Japan
note: Have reached the Round of 16 in three of the last four tournaments.

name: Sweden
note: Back at the World Cup for the first time since 2018.

name: Tunisia
note: Looking to escape the group stage for the first time.

name: Belgium
note: A new generation looks to build on the 'golden generation' era.

name: Iran
note: Face Belgium, Egypt and New Zealand in Group G.

name: Egypt
note: Back at the World Cup for the first time since 1990.

name: New Zealand
note: Qualified via the inter-confederation playoff.

name: Spain
note: FIFA's top-ranked side enter as a leading favorite.

name: Uruguay
note: Two-time champions in a tricky group with Spain.

name: Saudi Arabia
note: Co-hosts of 2034 — building momentum ahead of their home tournament.

name: Cape Verde
note: The smallest African nation ever to qualify.

name: France
note: Reigning runners-up, headlined by the Golden Boot favorite.

name: Norway
note: First World Cup since 1998, built around their captain.

name: Senegal
note: 2002 quarter-finalists return for another deep run.

name: Iraq
note: First World Cup appearance since 1986.

name: Argentina
note: Defending champions begin their title defence against Algeria.

name: Austria
note: A dangerous, well-organized side in Group J.

name: Algeria
note: Face defending champions Argentina in their opener.

name: Jordan
note: First-ever World Cup appearance.

name: Portugal
note: Ronaldo's record sixth World Cup, leading a deep squad.

name: Colombia
note: Looking to build on their 2014 quarter-final run.

name: Uzbekistan
note: First-ever World Cup appearance.

name: DR Congo
note: First World Cup appearance since 1974 (as Zaire).

name: England
note: Open against Croatia — a rematch of the 2018 semi-final.

name: Croatia
note: Three-time podium finishers in the last three tournaments.

name: Ghana
note: The 2010 quarter-finalists return to the tournament.

name: Panama
note: Looking to reach the knockout stage for the first time.
